description_long: This is a compact bungalow with a prominent, steeply pitched hip roof in a deep blue color, giving it a sturdy and weather-resistant appearance. The front facade features a small set of stairs leading up to a recessed entryway, which is flanked by a pair of simple, vertical posts and a small porch area. To the left of the entrance, there is a cluster of large, angular rocks, while the right side is adorned with a few slender bamboo stalks, adding a touch of natural landscaping. The walls are a muted beige, contrasting with the darker roof and trim, and the overall structure is low and wide, typical of bungalow proportions. The rear and side views show minimal window openings, emphasizing privacy and a solid, enclosed feel. The design is simple and functional, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on geometric forms.
description: A compact, hip-roofed bungalow with a small porch, beige walls, and minimal windows, accented by rocks and bamboo landscaping.
description_view_0: Front view showing the entry stairs, porch, bamboo stalks on the right, and a cluster of rocks on the left.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the hip roof, bamboo stalks, and partial view of the rocks; no windows visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain back wall and the full expanse of the hip roof, with no visible windows or features.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the hip roof and the cluster of rocks, with minimal architectural detail.
attribute_window_arrangement: minimal front windows, no visible side or rear windows
